name: Flow
domain: Fluid motion, laminar flow, vorticity, boundary layers
core_insight: Momentum and pressure trade off continuously. Flow is laminar until inertia overwhelms viscosity (Re >> 1); beyond that, order dissolves into turbulence. Vortices are the rotating memory of past flow.

key_equations.name: Navier-Stokes (Incompressible)
key_equations.why: The master equation of fluid motion: inertia balanced by pressure gradient and viscous diffusion

key_equations.name: Reynolds Number
key_equations.why: Single dimensionless number predicting laminar vs turbulent regime — the character of the flow

key_equations.name: Bernoulli's Equation
key_equations.why: Energy conservation along a streamline: speed up, pressure drops

seed_prompt: ↻ Trace the streamlines. Where does viscosity lose to inertia? The transition point is where complexity lives.

fourth_equation.name: Vorticity Equation
fourth_equation.why: Vorticity (rotation) is stretched and tilted by the flow itself — the mechanism behind energy cascade in turbulence

explore_paths.question: What happens when flow loses its order entirely?
explore_paths.why: Turbulence is high-Re flow past the breakdown point — F02 sets up the transition, F17 lives in the chaos beyond

explore_paths.question: What conservation law underlies flow continuity?
explore_paths.why: The continuity equation is the conservation-of-mass backbone of all fluid dynamics

explore_paths.question: How does flow carry information or life?
explore_paths.why: Metabolic networks, lymphatic systems, and ecosystems all use flow as their transport substrate

explore_paths.question: How do networks route flow optimally?
explore_paths.why: Graph-theoretic flow (max-flow/min-cut) is the discrete analog of fluid flow through networks

open_questions.question: Do smooth solutions to the Navier-Stokes equations always exist in 3D? (Millennium Prize Problem — $1M unsolved)
open_questions.tension: In 2D, smooth solutions are proven to exist. In 3D, it's unknown whether solutions can develop infinite velocities (blow-up) in finite time. Most physicists assume smoothness; mathematicians are not so sure.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: One of the seven Millennium Prize Problems. The gap between physical intuition ('flows stay smooth') and mathematical proof is a 150-year-old scandal in applied mathematics.

open_questions.question: Is there a deeper mathematical structure to turbulence, or is it irreducibly complex?
open_questions.tension: Kolmogorov's 1941 theory gave the -5/3 energy spectrum but assumed isotropy and homogeneity. Real turbulence is intermittent — energy is distributed in fractal bursts, not smoothly. Multifractal models fit better but have no first-principles derivation.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: Feynman called turbulence 'the most important unsolved problem in classical physics.' The question of whether it has an elegant deep structure or is genuinely irreducible is still wide open.

open_questions.question: Does the brain use fluid dynamics (the glymphatic system) as a cleaning and information-routing mechanism during sleep?
open_questions.tension: The glymphatic system (discovered ~2013) flushes cerebrospinal fluid through brain tissue during sleep, clearing metabolic waste. Whether this also serves an information-routing or memory-consolidation function is actively debated.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If the brain 'thinks' partly through fluid flow dynamics, the boundary between F02 and F16 may be much blurrier than assumed.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Blood flow is a fluid dynamics problem; turbulent flow in arteries causes damage and plaque formation
cross_domain_bridges.example: Stenosis creates turbulence downstream; Reynolds number predicts where plaques form

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Vehicle traffic follows flow equations; jams are phase transitions analogous to laminar-to-turbulent transition
cross_domain_bridges.example: Traffic density above critical threshold → stop-and-go waves that propagate backward without cause

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Goods, money, and information flow through networks with conservation laws and bottlenecks
cross_domain_bridges.example: The bullwhip effect in supply chains is a fluid-like oscillation amplified upstream

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Jet streams, ocean currents, and weather systems are all governed by Navier-Stokes at planetary scale
cross_domain_bridges.example: Rossby waves in the jet stream follow the same vorticity equations as laboratory flows

noise_signature.pattern: Stagnation — flow velocity drops to zero upstream of an obstacle
noise_signature.diagnosis: Boundary layer separation; the system is hitting a structural obstacle it cannot navigate around

noise_signature.pattern: Recirculation zones — flow reverses locally behind obstacles
noise_signature.diagnosis: Energy is being trapped rather than transported; vortex shedding may begin

noise_signature.pattern: Cavitation — vapor bubbles form in fast-moving low-pressure regions
noise_signature.diagnosis: Bernoulli's equation taken to an extreme; the system is tearing itself apart at pressure minima
